title: GLEIF Global LEI Registry
short_description: Global registry of 2.5M+ legal entities with unique 20-character ISO 17442 identifiers used across 300+ financial regulations.
long_description: GLEIF (Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation) maintains the Global LEI Index — the central repository of all legal entities participating in financial transactions. Contains over 2.5 million active LEI records across 200+ jurisdictions. Each LEI links to standardised reference data including legal names, registered addresses, operational headquarters, and ownership hierarchies (Level 2 data). Updated three times daily via Golden Copy Files and REST API. Mandatorily used in MiFID II, EMIR, Dodd-Frank, and 300+ global regulations for derivatives, securities, and corporate transactions. Essential for KYC/AML enrichment.
access_method: Free bulk download; Free API — no registration required
schema_fields: LEI|string|20-character ISO 17442 legal entity identifier;LegalName|string|Primary registered legal name;RegisteredAddress|object|Legally registered address;HeadquartersAddress|object|Principal operating address;LegalJurisdiction|string|ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 jurisdiction code;EntityStatus|string|ACTIVE or INACTIVE;RegistrationAuthority|string|LEI issuing organisation
sample_rows: 5493001KJTIIGC8Y1R12|Apple Inc.|Cupertino CA USA|Cupertino CA USA|US|ACTIVE|R0|RA000585;894500CJWJY90WZQ5L95|Amazon.com Inc.|Seattle WA USA|Seattle WA USA|US|ACTIVE;2138002AXVXVXAXJ2601|Nestlé S.A.|Vevey Switzerland|Vevey Switzerland|CH|ACTIVE
tags: legal entity identifier,lei,kyc,aml,corporate registry,mifid ii,emir,derivatives,global,iso 17442
notes: Golden Copy Files updated 3x daily at gleif.org/en/lei-data/gleif-golden-copy. Also available via goldencopy.gleif.org/api/v2/. Level 2 (ownership hierarchy) data also free. Used for regulatory reporting in MiFID II, EMIR, Dodd-Frank.

title: ESMA FIRDS — Financial Instruments Reference Data System
short_description: Daily XML registry of 1M+ financial instruments with ISIN, CFI code, MIC, and issuer LEI under MiFID II obligations.
long_description: ESMA's Financial Instruments Reference Data System publishes standardised reference metadata for all financial instruments in scope of MiFID II. Published daily at 09:00 CET in ISO 20022 XML format. Contains ISIN, Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI) code, Market Identifier Code (MIC), and issuer LEI for equities, bonds, derivatives, and structured products. Sourced from trading venues, systematic internalisers, and national competent authorities. Files split when exceeding 500,000 records. Used for market surveillance, transaction reporting, and regulatory compliance across EU/EEA.
access_method: Free bulk download via ESMA Registers portal
schema_fields: ISIN|string|International Securities Identification Number;CFI|string|Classification of Financial Instruments code;MIC|string|Market Identifier Code (4 chars);LegalName|string|Name of issuing entity;LEI|string|Legal Entity Identifier of issuer;TransparencyRequired|boolean|Pre-trade transparency obligation;PostTradeTransparent|boolean|Post-trade transparency status
tags: financial instruments,isin,mifid ii,instrument reference,cfi,market data,eea,derivatives,bonds,equities
notes: Published daily at 09:00 CET. ISO 20022 XML schema. Full Files daily; Delta Files for changes. Files >500k records split into parts. Technical spec ESMA65-8-5014.

title: ESMA FITRS — Financial Instruments Transparency System
short_description: Quarterly MiFID II pre/post-trade transparency thresholds (LIS, SSTI) and bond liquidity assessments by ISIN.
long_description: ESMA's Financial Instruments Transparency System publishes annual and quarterly transparency calculations determining pre-trade and post-trade transparency thresholds for equity and non-equity instruments under MiFID II. Contains Large In Scale (LIS) and Size Specific To Instrument (SSTI) thresholds at ISIN level. Includes bond market liquidity assessments updated quarterly. Results published in XML format. Used by trading venues and firms to determine transparency obligations for equity, bonds, derivatives, and structured products.
access_method: Free bulk download via ESMA Registers portal
schema_fields: ISIN|string|International Securities Identification Number;TransparencyType|string|Equity or NonEquity;PreTradeThreshold|decimal|LIS/SSTI pre-trade threshold EUR;PostTradeThreshold|decimal|Post-trade threshold;BondLiquidity|string|Liquid or Illiquid assessment;UpdateDate|date|Publication date;EffectiveDate|date|When threshold becomes effective
tags: transparency thresholds,mifid ii,lis,ssti,bond liquidity,pre-trade,post-trade,eea,isin,mifir
notes: Annual calculations published by 30 April. Quarterly bond liquidity updates. Daily delta files when changes occur; weekly full files. Technical spec ESMA65-8-5240.

title: FCA Market Data Processor — UK Transaction Reporting
short_description: UK regulatory system processing 30M+ daily transaction and order reports under UK MiFID II obligations post-Brexit.
long_description: The FCA's Market Data Processor is the UK's central transaction reporting system for MiFID II compliance post-Brexit. Processes 30+ million transaction reports and 100+ million order reports daily from investment firms submitted via Approved Reporting Mechanisms or direct connection. Contains ISIN, venue MIC, trade price, notional value, buyer/seller LEI, execution time, and order flags per trade. Data extraction available to submitting firms via MDP entity portal for reconciliation. Replaced ESMA transaction reporting for UK firms from 31 December 2020.
access_method: Regulated access via Approved Reporting Mechanisms or direct FCA MDP connection
schema_fields: ISIN|string|International Securities Identification Number;VenueMIC|string|ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code;TradePrice|decimal|Execution price;NotionalValue|decimal|Trade notional amount;NotionalCurrency|string|ISO 4217 currency code;BuyerLEI|string|ISO 17442 buyer identifier;SellerLEI|string|Seller identifier;ExecutionTime|timestamp|Trade execution timestamp UTC
tags: transaction reporting,mifid ii,mifir,uk regulation,market abuse,order reporting,fca,post-brexit,arms
notes: Access requires MIS confidentiality agreement. XSD schema v2.0. Transaction Reporting User Pack (TRUP) v3.1 specification. Architecture spans multiple ARMs and direct connections.

title: UK Debt Management Office — Gilt Auction Results
short_description: Historical UK government bond (gilt) auction results with yields, prices, amounts, and subscription ratios since April 1998.
long_description: The UK Debt Management Office maintains comprehensive records of all gilt operations since April 1998, including outright auctions, syndications, and tenders. Data includes auction dates, amounts issued, weighted average accepted yields and prices, maturity details, coupon rates, subscription ratios and tails. Over 650+ auction events. Used by fixed income portfolio managers, traders, central banks, and researchers for gilt market analysis and debt management monitoring.
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: AuctionDate|date|Date of auction;MaturityDate|date|Gilt maturity date;Coupon|decimal|Annual coupon rate %;Amount|decimal|Amount issued £ millions;Yield|decimal|Weighted average accepted yield %;Price|decimal|Weighted average accepted price;Tail|decimal|Difference between lowest and average yield bps;Subscription_Ratio|decimal|Total bids divided by amount on offer;Maturity_Type|string|Conventional/index-linked/STRIPS
tags: gilts,bonds,gilt auction,dmo,yield,fixed income,uk government debt,capital markets,treasury
notes: Historical yield data extends to April 1998. Multiple export formats. Also covers index-linked auctions. Essential for fixed income PMs and macro hedge funds.

title: Office for Budget Responsibility — Economic and Fiscal Forecasts Database
short_description: UK's official independent forecasts of ~100 economic and fiscal variables with 5-year projections, updated twice yearly.
long_description: The OBR publishes twice-yearly economic and fiscal forecasts (March and November) containing projections for ~100 economic and fiscal variables including GDP growth, CPI inflation, unemployment, tax revenues, public spending, PSNB, and national debt. Database includes all OBR forecasts since 2010, plus historical Treasury forecasts back to 1970. Covers quarterly and annual forecasts for 5-year horizon with uncertainty bands. Downloadable as ZIP containing CSV, JSON metadata, and README. R package 'obr' also available.
date_coverage: 1970 to present (forecast horizon to 5 years ahead)
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: Forecast_Date|date|Date forecast was published;Variable_Code|string|Unique code for economic variable;Variable_Name|string|Full name of variable;Year|integer|Fiscal or calendar year;Forecast_Value|decimal|Forecast value;Unit|string|Unit of measurement;Data_Type|string|Forecast/outturn/estimate;Scenario|string|Central/upside/downside
tags: fiscal policy,gdp,inflation,public finances,borrowing,debt,tax,spending,uk economy,macroeconomic forecast
notes: R package 'obr' provides programmatic access. Uncertainty bands alongside central forecasts. Contains historical Treasury forecasting data back to 1970.

title: Bank of England — Spot and Forward Gilt Yield Curves
short_description: Daily UK zero-coupon yield curves (nominal and real, spot and forward) derived from gilt prices since 1979.
long_description: Bank of England publishes daily zero-coupon yield curves derived from UK gilt and index-linked gilt prices. Six curve types: nominal spot, nominal forward, real spot, real forward, implied inflation spot, implied inflation forward. Nominal data from 2 January 1979; real from 2 January 1985. Maturity points at monthly intervals to 5 years, semi-annual to 25 years. Over 12,500 daily observations. Published by noon next business day. Essential for fixed income pricing, derivatives valuation, and macroeconomic analysis.
date_coverage: 2 January 1979 to present (nominal); 2 January 1985 to present (real)
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: Date|date|Yield curve calculation date;Maturity_1M|decimal|1-month zero coupon yield %;Maturity_3M|decimal|3-month yield %;Maturity_1Y|decimal|1-year yield %;Maturity_5Y|decimal|5-year yield %;Maturity_10Y|decimal|10-year yield %;Maturity_25Y|decimal|25-year yield %;Curve_Type|string|Nominal/Real/Implied inflation
tags: yield curve,interest rates,gilts,bonds,fixed income,spot rates,forward rates,monetary policy,bank of england
notes: Archive data as downloadable ZIP. Contact yield.curve@bankofengland.co.uk. Methodology in Bank working papers. Based on GC repo rates short end; gilt prices longer end.

title: PRA — Pillar 3 Bank Capital and Leverage Disclosures
short_description: Quarterly regulatory capital, leverage, and liquidity disclosures for UK authorised banks and building societies under Basel III.
long_description: PRA-authorised UK banks, building societies, and investment firms publish quarterly Pillar 3 disclosures under Capital Requirements Regulation. Template KM1 (key regulatory metrics) shows available capital tiers, buffer requirements, risk-weighted assets, leverage ratios, LCR, and NSFR. Currently ~20 major UK banks including HSBC, Barclays, Lloyds, NatWest, and Santander reporting. Data published quarterly on rolling 4-quarter basis within firm earnings releases and formal regulatory filings. Used by credit analysts, regulators, and institutional investors.
provider: Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) / Bank of England
access_method: Free bulk download (individual firm disclosures); aggregated in BoE Financial Stability Reports
schema_fields: Institution_Name|string|Full legal name;Reporting_Period|date|Quarter end date;CET1_Ratio|decimal|Common Equity Tier 1 ratio %;Tier1_Ratio|decimal|Tier 1 capital ratio %;Total_Capital_Ratio|decimal|Total capital ratio %;Leverage_Ratio|decimal|Leverage ratio %;LCR|decimal|Liquidity Coverage Ratio %;NSFR|decimal|Net Stable Funding Ratio %;RWA|decimal|Risk Weighted Assets £ millions
tags: bank capital,leverage ratio,pillar 3,prudential regulation,risk weighted assets,cet1,lcr,nsfr,uk banks,basel iii
notes: Data published by individual firms. PRA provides XBRL taxonomy v3.3.0. Some data commercially sensitive — aggregated available via BoE Financial Stability Report.

title: ONS / HM Treasury — Public Sector Net Borrowing Monthly Data
short_description: Monthly public sector net borrowing (PSNB) and net debt (PSND) outturn data for the UK from March 1993 to present.
long_description: ONS publishes monthly public sector finances statistical bulletins jointly with HM Treasury showing Public Sector Net Borrowing (PSNB) and Net Debt (PSND) excluding public sector banks. Monthly data from March 1993; quarterly back to 1946 for some series. Coverage includes central government receipts and spending, local authorities, and public corporations. ~960 monthly observations. Published ~4-6 weeks after reference month. Essential for fiscal policy monitoring, budget analysis, and investor assessment of UK sovereign risk.
provider: Office for National Statistics (ONS) / HM Treasury
access_method: Free bulk download; NOMIS API
schema_fields: Period|string|Period identifier e.g. Mar-2026;PSNB_ex|decimal|Public sector net borrowing excl public sector banks £ millions;PSND_ex|decimal|Net debt excl public sector banks £ millions;Central_Govt_Receipts|decimal|Central government revenue £ millions;Central_Govt_Spending|decimal|Central government spending £ millions;Current_Balance|decimal|Public sector current balance £ millions
tags: psnb,psnd,government borrowing,government debt,fiscal policy,public finances,uk economy,public spending,ons
notes: Available through NOMIS API for programmatic access. Revisions occur in subsequent months. Seasonal adjustments applied where noted.

title: UK Sanctions List (FCDO)
short_description: Official UK list of all designated individuals, entities, and vessels subject to financial and other sanctions under UK law.
long_description: The UK Sanctions List is the authoritative source for all UK sanctions designations under the Sanctions and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2018. Replaced the OFSI Consolidated List on 28 January 2026. Covers 25+ sanctions regimes including Russia, Iran, North Korea, Syria, Belarus, Myanmar, and others. Contains individuals, entities, and specified vessels subject to asset freezes and other restrictive measures. Available in 7 machine-readable formats with real-time updates. Mandatory screening requirement for all UK financial institutions.
format: CSV, XML, JSON, Excel, PDF, HTML, TXT
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: unique_id|string|Unique designee identifier;individual_name|string|Full name of designated individual;entity_name|string|Business or organisation name;date_of_birth|date|Birth date if applicable;nationality|string|Country of citizenship or registration;designation_regime|string|Sanctions regime e.g. Russia/Iran;asset_freeze|boolean|Whether asset freeze applies;last_updated|date|Date of last record update
tags: sanctions,financial regulation,designated entities,asset freeze,compliance,aml,counter-terrorism,ofsi,fcdo,screening
notes: Search tool at search-uk-sanctions-list.service.gov.uk. OpenSanctions also maintains the dataset at opensanctions.org/datasets/gb_fcdo_sanctions/ in JSON/CSV. Replaced OFSI Consolidated List January 2026.

title: FCA Financial Services Register
short_description: Official register of all UK-authorised and regulated financial services firms and individuals with permissions and regulatory history.
long_description: Official public register of all firms, individuals, and other bodies currently or previously authorised by the FCA and/or PRA. Includes regulatory permissions, disciplinary records, fines, and enforcement history. Also covers the Directory of certified/assessed persons under the Senior Managers and Certification Regime (SMCR). 33,000+ organisations. Searchable by firm name, individual name, or permission type. Register Extract Service (RES) provides bulk pipe-delimited text download (~17 files, ~100MB) updated weekly.
format: JSON, REST API, Pipe-delimited text (Register Extract Service)
access_method: Free API — registration required; Subscription bulk download (RES)
schema_fields: firm_reference|string|FCA unique identifier;firm_name|string|Legal trading name;permission|string|Regulated activity permissions;authorization_status|string|Authorised or previously authorised;address|object|Registered address;individuals|array|Associated key individuals;disciplinary_record|object|Fines and enforcement actions
tags: fca register,authorised firms,regulated individuals,financial regulation,compliance,smcr,senior managers,permissions,uk financial services
notes: REST API rate limit 50 req/10 sec. Register Extract Service: 17 pipe-delimited files with ~59-page Subscribers' Handbook. Weekly updates. Contact registerapisupport@fca.org.uk.

title: FCA Authorised Funds Register
short_description: Registry of all UK-authorised collective investment schemes (unit trusts, OEICs, UCITs, AIFs) with manager and status data.
long_description: The FCA register contains comprehensive data on all ~4,800 UK-authorised funds and collective investment schemes. Includes authorised unit trusts, OEICs, UCITS, alternative investment funds, and authorised contractual schemes. Each record includes fund name, FCA reference number, authorisation status, manager details, fund type, and base currency. Available via FCA Register REST API (free, registration required) or Register Extract Service (bulk, subscription). Weekly updates every Thursday.
access_method: Free API — registration required; Register Extract Service (subscription)
schema_fields: fund_name|string|Official scheme name;fca_reference_number|string|Unique FCA reference;isin_code|string|ISIN identifier if available;fund_type|string|Unit trust/OEIC/UCITS/AIF;manager_name|string|Fund manager name;base_currency|string|Currency of financial reports;authorization_status|string|Authorised or Recognised;date_authorised|date|Authorisation date
tags: fca,authorised funds,collective investment schemes,ucits,aif,unit trusts,oeics,fund data,uk funds,smcr
notes: API: financial-services-register-api.readthedocs.io. Python package 'fsrapiclient' on PyPI. RES via Spectrum Data Management (SDM). Rate limit 10 req/10 sec.

title: Investment Association — Monthly UK Fund Industry Statistics
short_description: Monthly aggregated UK fund industry AUM, net flows, and sector sales data covering 4,800+ FCA-authorised collective investment schemes.
long_description: The Investment Association publishes comprehensive monthly statistics on UK investment fund industry activity covering funds under management, retail and institutional sales, sector breakdowns, and monthly company rankings. Tracks ~4,800 IA-classified funds across unit trusts and OEICs. Includes net flows by sector, AUM trends, performance rankings, and industry metrics. Available in Excel for IA members with historical data back to 1992 available for purchase. Published monthly with one-month lag.
licence: Commercial — member access; Historical data purchase available
access_method: Free (members only); Paid for non-members; Contact statistics@theia.org
schema_fields: fund_name|string|Name of investment fund;sector|string|IA fund classification sector;sales_month|decimal|Monthly sales £ millions;assets_under_management|decimal|AUM £ millions;net_flows|decimal|Net sales flows £ millions;performance_1yr|decimal|12-month performance %;performance_3yr|decimal|3-year performance %;fund_manager|string|Management company name
tags: investment association,fund statistics,aum,fund sales,sector data,performance,uk funds,retail investment,net flows
notes: Non-members can purchase historical data (1992+) from statistics@theia.org. Monthly reports published ~one month in arrears. Negotiate commercial data licence.

title: UK Takeover Panel — Rule 8 Disclosure Table
short_description: Live database of all Rule 8 dealings and position disclosures during UK takeover offer periods, downloadable as CSV in real-time.
long_description: The Takeover Panel publishes a live disclosure table of all Rule 8 public position disclosures during active UK takeover offer periods. Rule 8 requires persons holding 1%+ of any class of relevant securities to disclose positions and dealings. Data includes discloser names, shareholdings, percentages, dealing dates, and ISIN codes. Direct CSV download available with real-time updates. Beta version includes LEI fields. Over 50 years of historical offer documentation maintained. Form 8.3 is the standard public disclosure form.
provider: The Panel on Takeovers and Mergers
record_count: Variable (hundreds of disclosures per active offer period)
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: offeree_company|string|Target company name;offeror_company|string|Bidding company name;discloser_name|string|Name of person making disclosure;shares_held|integer|Number of securities held;percentage_held|decimal|Percentage of relevant class;disclosure_date|date|Date of disclosure;dealing_date|date|Date dealing occurred;isin_code|string|ISIN of relevant securities;disclosure_type|string|Opening Position or Dealing Disclosure
tags: takeover,mergers and acquisitions,rule 8,disclosure,shareholding,dealing,m&a,uk market,form 8.3,offer period
notes: Direct CSV: https://www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk/new/disclosuretable/v3/disclosuretable.csv. XLS and XML also available. Beta version includes LEI fields. Market Surveillance Unit: +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

title: FCA Final Notices and Enforcement Database
short_description: Repository of all FCA Final Notices and enforcement actions detailing regulatory sanctions, fines, and outcomes since 1997.
long_description: The FCA publishes enforcement notices detailing formal regulatory actions against firms and individuals. Final Notices are issued when FCA action is concluded; Decision Notices when action is decided; Warning Notices when proposed. In 2024/25 the FCA issued 37 Final Notices, imposed £186m+ in fines, and cancelled authorisation of 1,456 firms. Records include firm name, reference number, sanction type, penalty amount, breach description and date. Searchable PDF database with structured enforcement data summaries published annually.
access_method: Free search and download (PDFs)
schema_fields: firm_name|string|Name of regulated firm or individual;fca_reference_number|string|FCA firm reference;notice_type|string|Final/Decision/Warning;penalty_amount|decimal|Fine in GBP;breach_description|string|Description of regulatory breach;enforcement_date|date|Date notice issued;section_violated|string|FCA Handbook section breached
tags: fca enforcement,final notices,regulatory action,penalties,fines,market conduct,money laundering,financial regulation,compliance
notes: FORMAT: PDF — requires AI extraction for structured data. Annual enforcement data summary published. 2024/25: 37 Final Notices, 5 criminal convictions, £186m fines. FCA Enforcement Watch newsletter launched January 2026.

title: CDP Corporate Climate and Water Disclosure Scores
short_description: Annual environmental disclosure scores (A–D) for 24,800 companies covering climate change, water security, and deforestation risk.
long_description: CDP collects and publishes environmental disclosure data from the world's largest companies and cities. In 2024, 24,800 companies disclosed through CDP, representing approximately two-thirds of global market capitalisation. Dataset includes climate change scores, water security scores, and forest scores on an A–D scale. Covers GHG emissions (Scopes 1, 2, 3), transition plans, physical risk assessments, and management practices. Historical data from 2003. Open Data Portal built on Socrata platform; REST API available. Non-commercial use under CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0.
access_method: Free API — registration required; Paid commercial data licensing
schema_fields: organization_id|string|Unique disclosing organization identifier;organization_name|string|Legal company name;disclosure_year|integer|Year of disclosure;climate_score|string|Climate change performance grade A/B/C/D;water_score|string|Water security grade;forest_score|string|Forest preservation grade;industry_sector|string|GICS sector;scope1_emissions|number|Direct GHG emissions tonnes CO2e;scope2_emissions|number|Purchased energy emissions;scope3_emissions|number|Value chain emissions
tags: climate disclosure,carbon emissions,esg,sustainability,scope 1 2 3,water,forests,tcfd,net zero,corporate reporting
notes: Non-commercial use only under CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0. Commercial licence required for fund/asset manager use — negotiate with partnerships@cdp.net. REST API requires subscription key. 2024 A-List: only 2% of disclosing companies.

title: Science Based Targets Initiative — Corporate Target Registry
short_description: Weekly-updated registry of 10,000+ companies with validated science-based net-zero and near-term GHG targets aligned to 1.5°C.
long_description: The SBTi Target Dashboard lists 10,000+ companies and 2,800+ financial institutions with validated science-based targets or formal commitments to develop targets within 24 months. Companies are assessed for temperature alignment (1.5°C, well-below 2°C, or 2°C) with scope-specific breakdowns. Dashboard updated weekly. Downloadable in XLS format with GICS sector mapping and data dictionary. Key input for portfolio decarbonisation analysis and net-zero alignment assessment by asset managers.
licence: UNKNOWN — limited to non-commercial educational and research use; check publisher terms
access_method: Free bulk download (XLS)
schema_fields: company_name|string|Legal name;sector|string|GICS sector;region|string|Geographic region;target_status|string|Targets Set or Committed;temperature_alignment|string|1.5C/Well-below 2C/2C;target_type|string|Near-term/Long-term/Net-zero;scope_1|boolean|Scope 1 covered;scope_2|boolean|Scope 2 covered;scope_3|boolean|Scope 3 covered;base_year|integer|GHG baseline year;target_year|integer|Target achievement year;validation_date|date|Date validated
tags: net zero,science based targets,climate commitments,scope emissions,temperature alignment,corporate climate,1.5 degrees,portfolio decarbonisation
notes: Dashboard redesigned 2025 with SBTi IDs for distinct business identification. Data dictionary on dashboard. Separate files: by-company and by-target. Negotiate commercial licence for fund use.

title: UNPRI — Responsible Investment Signatory Registry
short_description: Registry of 5,000+ institutional investors managing US$128 trillion AUM committed to UN Principles for Responsible Investment.
long_description: The UNPRI maintains a signatory directory and Data Portal with investment manager practices, asset allocation by class (fixed income, equity, real estate, infrastructure, private equity), and geographic distribution. Signatories represent 5,090+ organisations from 91+ countries with cumulative AUM of ~US$128 trillion. Annual reporting cycle. Covers ESG integration practices, active ownership, collaborative engagement, and policy advocacy across all major asset classes. Widely used for ESG manager due diligence.
licence: UNKNOWN — contact info@unpri.org; Data Portal requires login
access_method: Free web directory; Data Portal login required; Contact for bulk data
schema_fields: signatory_name|string|Organisation legal name;signatory_type|string|Asset owner/Manager/Service provider;hq_country|string|Headquarters country;hq_region|string|Geographic region;aum_usd_billions|decimal|Assets under management USD billions;asset_classes|string|Covered asset classes;first_signature_year|integer|Year of signing;signatory_status|string|Active or Delisted
tags: responsible investment,esg,institutional investors,pension funds,asset owners,aum,pri,sustainability,net zero,active ownership
notes: Signatory directory download: email info@unpri.org. Data Portal at dataportal.unpri.org. Historical data (2014-2020) for academics via reporting@unpri.org. Negotiate data licence for commercial use.

title: Environment Agency Pollution Inventory
short_description: Annual facility-level industrial pollution releases in England covering air emissions, water discharges, and waste transfers since 1998.
long_description: The Environment Agency Pollution Inventory collects annual mass releases of specified substances from large regulated industrial sites in England. Covers releases to air, controlled waters, sewers, and off-site waste transfers. Data spans 1998 to 2024 with downloadable CSV files. Includes facility name, location, industrial classification, substance names and release quantities. Aligns with EU Pollutant Release and Transfer Register framework. Used for environmental due diligence, ESG portfolio screening, and regulatory compliance analysis.
record_count: UNKNOWN — thousands of release records annually
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: facility_name|string|Industrial site registered name;facility_id|string|Unique facility identifier;postcode|string|UK postcode;industrial_classification|string|IPPC/EPR activity code;substance_name|string|Pollutant substance;cas_number|string|CAS Registry number;release_to_air|decimal|Annual mass release to air tonnes;release_to_water|decimal|Annual mass to water tonnes;waste_transferred|decimal|Off-site waste transfer tonnes;reporting_year|integer|Calendar year
tags: industrial pollution,air emissions,water discharge,waste transfer,prtr,facility level,hazardous substances,environment,esg,physical risk
notes: Also mirrored at environment.data.gov.uk. EA Conditional Licence: review permitted uses. Reporting deadline 28 February annually. Useful for ESG physical risk and supply chain analysis.

title: HM Treasury — UK Green Financing Allocation Reports
short_description: Annual reports tracking £43.4 billion in green gilt and savings bond proceeds allocated to 39+ eligible government green expenditures.
long_description: HM Treasury publishes annual allocation reports tracking proceeds from green gilt issuance via the Debt Management Office and NS&I Green Savings Bonds directed to eligible green expenditures. As of October 2024, £43.4 billion raised since September 2021: £41.6B via gilts, £1.8B via bonds. 2023-24 allocated £10.8B to new green expenditures. Categories include climate change mitigation (renewable energy, energy efficiency), natural capital restoration, and low-carbon transport. Published annually in October.
provider: HM Treasury / UK Debt Management Office
access_method: Free bulk download
schema_fields: expenditure_name|string|Official project/programme name;ministry_department|string|Responsible department;expenditure_category|string|Climate mitigation/Natural capital/Low-carbon transport;allocation_2023_24_gbp|decimal|2023-24 allocation £ millions;cumulative_prior_gbp|decimal|Prior year cumulative £ millions;green_gilt_proceeds|decimal|Funded by green gilts £ millions;environmental_impact|string|Environmental outcome description
tags: green bonds,green gilts,climate finance,sustainable finance,net zero,renewable energy,environmental expenditure,esg,sovereign bonds
notes: FORMAT: PDF — requires AI extraction for structured data. Annual reports published October. Biennial environmental impact reporting. Green Financing Framework updated November 2025.

title: ICE Futures Europe — Daily Settlement Prices
short_description: Daily settlement prices for Brent crude oil, TTF natural gas, and European power futures contracts on ICE Futures Europe.
long_description: ICE Futures Europe publishes daily settlement prices for its benchmark energy contracts including Brent crude oil futures, Dutch TTF natural gas futures, and European power contracts. Settlement prices calculated as weighted average of trades in designated 2-minute settlement windows. Data includes open, high, low, close, settlement price and volume. Historical data from 2011 via ICE Report Centre. Essential pricing benchmark for energy-exposed portfolios, commodity trading desks, and macro hedge funds.
record_count: 3,500+ daily records per contract; 10+ years historical
access_method: Paid subscription via ICE Report Centre or SFTP; Third-party access via Databento, Barchart
schema_fields: contract_code|string|Product identifier e.g. BRN/TTF;settlement_date|date|Trading date YYYY-MM-DD;open_price|decimal|Opening price USD/bbl or USD/MWh;high_price|decimal|Intraday high;low_price|decimal|Intraday low;close_price|decimal|Weighted average settlement price;volume|integer|Total contracts traded;open_interest|integer|Open contract positions
tags: crude oil,natural gas,futures,energy markets,brent,ttf,settlement prices,commodities,derivatives,ice
notes: Requires ICE registration or third-party distributor. Settlement: 2-minute window from 19:28 London time. Consolidated Feed API available via ICE Developer Center. Negotiate licence for commercial use.

title: London Metal Exchange — Warrant and Inventory Data
short_description: Daily LME warehouse warrant holdings and inventory for base metals (copper, zinc, nickel, aluminium, lead, tin, cobalt) by location.
long_description: The London Metal Exchange publishes daily warrant holdings and inventory reports showing opening stocks, live and cancelled warrants, delivered in/out tonnage by metal, location, and country of origin. Covers 7 base metals across the global LME-approved warehouse network. Reports published at 09:00 UK time with 2-day delay. Includes stock breakdown reports, warrant banding reports, and country-of-origin analyses. Key signal for commodity supply/demand analysis and metals pricing. Historical archives available.
licence: Commercial — licence required; GBP 1,200/year for daily off-warrant data
record_count: 2,000+ daily records across metals and warehouses; 10+ years historical
access_method: Paid subscription via LMEsource, LMEselectMD; Third-party: Fastmarkets, Metals-API
schema_fields: metal_code|string|Metal identifier Cu/Zn/Ni/Al/Pb/Sn/Co;warehouse_location|string|Physical location;opening_stock|integer|Tonnage at period start;live_warrants|integer|Active warehouse warrants;cancelled_warrants|integer|Cancelled warrant count;delivered_in|integer|Tonnage received;delivered_out|integer|Tonnage removed;closing_stock|integer|Closing tonnage;country_of_origin|string|Commodity source country
tags: base metals,warehouse,inventory,warrants,copper,zinc,nickel,aluminium,lme,commodities,supply demand
notes: Negotiate LME market data licence at lme.com. Daily off-warrant stock reports (T+1) available to subscribers from April 2025. API available for LME members.

title: OFGEM — Retail and Wholesale Energy Price Indices
short_description: Quarterly indices tracking wholesale and retail electricity and gas prices, supplier costs, and market structure for Great Britain.
long_description: OFGEM's interactive data portal publishes wholesale and retail energy market indicators for Great Britain including retail price changes, supplier costs (Quarterly Supplier Cost Index), consumer complaints, and market structure metrics. Wholesale indicators cover price trends, security of supply, competition metrics, and investment. Data available from 2008. Every chart includes downloadable CSV. Used by energy traders, policy analysts, and any investment firm with energy sector exposure or energy transition mandates.
provider: Office of Gas and Electricity Markets (OFGEM)
format: CSV (per chart export), Interactive web portal
record_count: 100+ data series; thousands of quarterly observations from 2008
access_method: Free bulk download (CSV export per chart); Data Exchange service (beta API)
schema_fields: date|date|Quarter or period;metric_code|string|Index identifier;value|decimal|Index value or price pence/kWh;fuel_type|string|Electricity or Gas;supplier_count|integer|Active supplier count;market_segment|string|Domestic/SME/Industrial
tags: energy prices,wholesale,retail,price index,supplier costs,ofgem,electricity,gas,great britain,energy market
notes: New Data Exchange service (beta) offers API access. Historical from 2008. CSV export available per chart from web interface.

title: BVCA — Annual UK Private Equity and Venture Capital Industry Data
short_description: Annual survey data on UK private equity and VC fundraising, investment, and exits covering 1,600+ businesses and £29.4B deployed.
long_description: BVCA (British Private Equity and Venture Capital Association) publishes annual Investment Activity Survey covering fundraising, business investments, and exits by UK PE and VC firms. 2024 data: 1,600+ UK businesses supported with £29.4B deployed. Covers all investment types (buyouts, growth, VC), company sizes, industry sectors, and UK regions. Based on ~200 BVCA member responses supplemented by public sources. Excel chartbook data available for download. Closest freely available equivalent to Preqin/PitchBook for UK private markets.
provider: BVCA (British Private Equity and Venture Capital Association)
licence: UNKNOWN — check publisher terms; contact BVCA for commercial licensing
record_count: 500-800 transaction records per year; 15+ years annual data
access_method: Free download of annual reports and Excel files
schema_fields: year|integer|Reporting year;investment_type|string|Buyout/Growth/Venture Capital;company_size|string|Small/Medium/Large/Megadeals;investment_amount_gbp|decimal|Deployment £ millions;number_of_transactions|integer|Deal count;sector|string|Industry classification;region|string|UK region;stage|string|Early/Growth/Late
tags: private equity,venture capital,uk,investment,fundraising,exits,pe,vc,alternative assets,dealflow
notes: Annual reports published May/June. Performance Measurement Survey also published annually with cash flow and valuation data. No public API. Negotiate commercial data licence directly.

title: British Business Bank — SME Finance Markets Report Data
short_description: Annual survey of 1,700+ UK SMEs on access to finance, demand, awareness, and credit experiences across all financing types.
long_description: The British Business Bank conducts an annual Business Finance Survey (via Ipsos) covering 1,700-1,750 UK SMEs on access to finance, demand for credit, experiences seeking finance, and awareness of options. The 2025 survey covered 1,734 businesses. Includes SME breakdowns by size, region, sector, and financing type (bank loans, overdrafts, equity, grants). Annual Small Business Finance Markets Report is now in its 10th edition. Useful for understanding SME credit demand as an economic leading indicator.
licence: UNKNOWN — check publisher terms; reports publicly available for download
access_method: Free download (annual reports); Contact BBB for granular data
schema_fields: survey_year|integer|Year conducted;business_size|string|Micro/Small/Medium;sector|string|Industry classification;region|string|UK region;finance_sought|boolean|Sought finance in past 12 months;finance_type|string|Bank loan/overdraft/equity/grant;success_rate|decimal|Percentage obtaining finance;awareness_score|decimal|Awareness of finance options %
tags: sme,small business,finance,lending,credit,uk economy,survey,business bank,alternative finance,equity
notes: Annual reports published online as free PDFs. No public API. Contact British Business Bank for bulk data access or commercial arrangements.

title: Companies House — XBRL Financial Accounts Bulk Download
short_description: Free daily bulk download of 5,000+ electronically filed UK company accounts in structured iXBRL/XBRL format.
long_description: Companies House provides daily and monthly bulk downloads of electronically filed company accounts in iXBRL and XBRL format. Approximately 5,000 filings processed daily (~75% of 2.2 million annual filings). Monthly archives contain nested HTML (iXBRL) and XML (XBRL) files organised by company number and balance sheet date with ~60 variables per record. Free FTP access updated daily. Enables large-scale extraction of structured financial data for credit analysis, market research, and economic monitoring.
access_method: Free bulk download — FTP
schema_fields: company_number|string|8-digit company identifier;balance_sheet_date|date|Financial period end date;filing_date|date|Date filed with Companies House;company_name|string|Legal company name;filing_format|string|iXBRL or XBRL;accounts_category|string|Small/medium/large/other;turnover|decimal|Revenue £;profit_loss|decimal|Profit or loss £;net_assets|decimal|Net assets £;employees|integer|Employee count
tags: company accounts,financial statements,xbrl,ixbrl,companies house,balance sheet,profit and loss,structured finance data,uk companies
notes: Daily files cover last 60 days; monthly archives 12 months plus historical. Only electronically filed accounts (~75% coverage). FTP: download.companieshouse.gov.uk/en_monthlyaccountsdata.html for monthly archives.

title: Companies House — Director Disqualification Register
short_description: Register of individuals banned from acting as UK company directors under the Company Directors Disqualification Act 1986.
long_description: Public register of individuals disqualified from acting as directors or members of Limited Liability Partnerships. Approximately 1,000-1,200 directors disqualified annually (range 802-1,222 in 2020-2024). Records include disqualification start and end dates, type, court details, reasons, and permissions to act. Searchable by name via web interface and accessible via REST API. Records auto-expire on database upon end of disqualification. Essential for company officer due diligence and compliance.
access_method: Free API — registration required; Free web search
schema_fields: person_number|string|Unique person identifier;person_name|string|Name of disqualified person;disqualified_from|date|Start date;disqualified_until|date|End date;disqualification_type|string|Type of order;case_identifier|string|Court case reference;court_name|string|Issuing court;reason|string|Act/section basis for disqualification
tags: director disqualification,banned directors,company law,corporate governance,companies house,compliance,uk business registry,due diligence
notes: API endpoint: GET /company/{company_number}/disqualified-officers. Duration ranges 2-15 years by offence severity. CSV dataset on data.gov.uk. Records removed automatically at expiry.

title: Insolvency Service — Individual Insolvency Register
short_description: Real-time register of all bankruptcies, Debt Relief Orders, and Individual Voluntary Arrangements in England and Wales.
long_description: Searchable register combining individual insolvency, bankruptcy restrictions, and debt relief restrictions registers for England and Wales. Contains bankruptcy orders, Debt Relief Orders (DROs), Individual Voluntary Arrangements (IVAs), and restrictions orders. Approximately 10,500 monthly insolvencies — 599 bankruptcies, 4,001 DROs, and 5,915 IVAs (July 2025 data). Real-time updates via web interface. Monthly statistics downloadable as CSV. Records retained approximately 3 months after discharge. Critical for individual credit risk and KYC.
access_method: Free web search; Free CSV statistics download
schema_fields: individual_name|string|Full name;insolvency_type|string|Bankruptcy/DRO/IVA;insolvency_date|date|Date of order;discharge_date|date|Date of discharge;court_name|string|Handling court;case_number|string|Reference number;insolvency_status|string|Active or discharged
tags: insolvency,bankruptcy,debt relief,iva,individual voluntary arrangement,england wales,credit risk,kyc,financial distress
notes: No bulk API — individual statistics published monthly as CSV on GOV.UK. Active register searchable by name or sole trader trading name. Retained ~3 months after discharge.

title: Companies House — Charges Register
short_description: Register of all secured charges (mortgages, debentures, pledges) registered against UK company assets, with satisfaction status.
long_description: Registry of charges (mortgages, security interests, pledges, debentures) registered against company property and assets. Includes details of secured lending: charge dates, parties entitled, particulars of charge, and satisfaction status. Accessible via REST API per company. Approximately 117,700 mortgage charges filed annually with ~58% satisfied each year. Provides complete security interest history for each company — critical for credit assessment, leveraged finance due diligence, and distressed debt analysis.
access_method: Free API — registration required
schema_fields: charge_number|string|Unique charge identifier;charge_code|string|Type of charge;acquired_on|date|Date charge created;satisfied_on|date|Date charge discharged;charge_holder|string|Parties entitled to the charge;particulars|string|Details of secured asset;classification|string|Charge classification;created_on|date|Registration date with Companies House
tags: charges,mortgages,secured lending,debentures,company assets,liens,credit,leveraged finance,due diligence,companies house
notes: API: GET /company/{company_number}/charges. Requires free API key. Real-time data. Key fields: acquired_on, charge_code, classification, particulars, persons_entitled, satisfied_on, created_on.
